articles.title: Rain, storm bring relief from heatwave across India: Sandstorm hits Jaisalmer, 50 rescued from flooded rive... - Bhaskar English
articles.search_term: heatwave (Almora OR Bageshwar OR Chamoli OR Champawat OR Dehradun OR Haridwar OR Nainital OR "Pauri Garhwal" OR Pithoragarh OR Rudraprayag OR "Tehri Garhwal" OR "Udham Singh Nagar" OR Uttarkashi) Uttarakhand
articles.full_text:
Monsoon to reach Keralam within 3 days:Heavy rain alert issued for 5 states, including MP, Raj; weather warnings issued for 63 districts in UP
New Delhi/Bhopal/Jaipur/Lucknow5 minutes ago
The India Meteorological Department (IMD) has announced that the South-western Monsoon in the country could reach Keralam within 2 to 3 days. The monsoon generally reaches the state by June 1, but it will be a bit late this time.
to declare an end to the prevailing heatwave conditions nationwide.
On Monday, rain was recorded in six districts of Rajasthan, including Kota. Hailstorms were also reported in Ajmer, Nagaur, and Chittorgarh. Meanwhile, four districts of Madhya Pradesh continued to experience strong winds and rainfall.
Heavy rain lashed Jhansi and Lalitpur in Uttar Pradesh. In Sambhal, a dust storm uprooted more than 50 trees and knocked down electricity poles. In Deoria, a woman died after being struck by lightning. Authorities have issued a thunderstorm and rain alert for 63 districts across the state. Meanwhile, Sasaram in Bihar received heavy rainfall during the afternoon.
Heavy rain alert issued for several states
Meanwhile, alerts for light to heavy rainfall have been issued in 27 states, including Jharkhand, Chhattisgarh, Haryana, and Punjab. Rainfall was also recorded in six districts of Uttarakhand, including Nainital.
Due to adverse weather conditions, all schools up to Class 12 remained closed in Pithoragarh. The Kedarnath pilgrimage was temporarily suspended. In Champawat, more than 50 pilgrims who were stranded in a swollen river were safely rescued by authorities.
Rajasthan sees 2nd day of dust storms
Dust storms continued across parts of Rajasthan for a second consecutive day, reducing visibility and lowering temperatures.
A sandstorm struck Jaisalmer on Sunday evening, creating near-dark conditions during daylight hours. Videos from the area showed massive clouds of dust moving across the desert landscape.
The maximum temperature in Phalodi, one of the state's hottest locations, settled at 42.6°C.
The state had already experienced a major dust storm on Saturday affecting five districts, Sri Ganganagar, Bikaner, Churu, Hanumangarh and Sikar.
The storm impacted an area of nearly 200 square kilometres and generated wind speeds of up to 56 kmph.
Weather pictures from the states
Due to rain in Champawat, Uttarakhand, the river current became strong. Security forces rescued 50 people
It rained on Sunday afternoon in Kolkata, West Bengal. Due to strong winds, the policeman's umbrella flipped over
Boatmen pushing a boat standing on the Sangam bank into the river in Prayag, Uttar Pradesh
Heavy rain occurred in Pushkar, Rajasthan on Sunday. This led to waterlogging in many places
Sandstorm in Rajasthan for the second consecutive day
A cloud of dust was seen rising in Jaisalmer, which slowly moved forward. A little later, darkness covered the area
Rain brings relief to Uttar Pradesh
Several districts in Uttar Pradesh received moderate rainfall. Ballia recorded 34.4 mm of rain, while Moradabad received 21.8 mm. Lucknow registered 2.4 mm of rainfall.
The maximum temperature in the state capital dropped to 36.3°C, nearly 4°C below normal, while the minimum temperature was recorded at 24.7°C.
Kedarnath Yatra temporarily halted
Heavy rainfall and adverse weather conditions disrupted pilgrimage activities in Uttarakhand. Authorities temporarily suspended the Kedarnath Yatra as a precautionary measure.
In Champawat district, more than 50 devotees attending the annual Jod Mela at Shri Reetha Sahib Gurudwara were stranded after a nearby river swelled due to rain. Rescue teams later evacuated all those trapped safely.
Monsoon expected to reach Kerala by June 4
The IMD has forecast that the southwest monsoon is likely to arrive in Kerala by June 4.
However, the weather agency has warned that the upcoming monsoon season may be weaker than normal. According to its forecast, India could receive around 78 cm of rainfall between June and September, below the long-term seasonal average of 87 cm.
While Bihar and Uttar Pradesh are expected to receive near-normal rainfall, several other regions may experience below-normal precipitation. Rain-fed agricultural zones are particularly vulnerable to potential rainfall deficits.
Weather forecast for June 2
Strong winds of 40–50 kmph are likely in parts of Punjab, Haryana, Delhi, Uttar Pradesh, Jharkhand and Rajasthan.
Maharashtra and Karnataka may witness thunderstorms accompanied by lightning and gusty winds.
Widespread rainfall is expected across Tamil Nadu, Puducherry, Kerala and Karnataka, with heavy showers possible in isolated areas.
Thunderstorms and rain are likely to continue across Assam, Meghalaya, Arunachal Pradesh, Nagaland, Manipur, Mizoram and Tripura.
Weather forecast for June 3
Thunderstorms accompanied by gusty winds of 40–50 kmph are expected across Punjab, Haryana, Delhi, Chandigarh, Uttar Pradesh and Rajasthan. Dust storms may also occur in some areas.
Madhya Pradesh, Chhattisgarh, Jharkhand and Bihar are likely to experience rain, lightning and strong winds.
Light to moderate rainfall is forecast across Kerala, Karnataka, Tamil Nadu, Puducherry and Andhra Pradesh, with isolated heavy rainfall along coastal regions.
Northeastern states are expected to continue receiving rain and thunderstorms, with some locations likely to witness heavy to very heavy rainfall.
Odisha and West Bengal may experience humid conditions along with scattered thunderstorms and light rain.

articles.title: Rain, Storms End Heatwave Across India; 50 Rescued - Dainik Jagran MP CG
articles.full_text:
Rain, Storms End Heatwave Across India; 50 Rescued
Digital Desk
Widespread rain and dust storms ended India’s heatwave. Sandstorm hit Jaisalmer, 50 rescued from flooded river in Uttarakhand. IMD forecasts weaker monsoon from June 4.
Rainfall, thunderstorms and cloud cover brought a decisive end to the intense heatwave that had gripped large parts of India, with the India Meteorological Department (IMD) confirming on Sunday that heatwave conditions have subsided nationwide.
Temperatures dropped sharply across several states as active weather systems triggered rain, strong winds and dust storms. While the change brought widespread relief from weeks of scorching heat, it also caused travel disruptions and affected religious events in some regions.
Heatwave finally breaks
After prolonged spells of 45°C-plus temperatures in many areas, Sunday’s weather shift offered a visible respite. The IMD said the combination of moisture-laden winds and western disturbances led to the sudden change.
Officials noted that maximum temperatures in previously heatwave-hit zones fell by 4 to 6 degrees Celsius compared to earlier in the week.
Rajasthan sees second dust storm
Parts of Rajasthan witnessed dust storms for a second consecutive day. On Sunday evening, a thick sandstorm hit Jaisalmer, turning daylight into near-darkness within minutes. Videos circulating from the area showed towering clouds of dust rolling across the desert landscape.
Phalodi, one of Rajasthan’s hottest towns, recorded a relatively lower maximum of 42.6°C. The state had already experienced a major dust storm on Saturday that affected Sri Ganganagar, Bikaner, Churu, Hanumangarh and Sikar – an area of nearly 200 square kilometres – with wind speeds reaching 56 kmph.
50 rescued from swollen river in Uttarakhand
In Uttarakhand’s Champawat district, more than 50 devotees attending the annual Jod Mela at Shri Reetha Sahib Gurudwara were stranded after a nearby river swelled due to heavy rain. Local authorities and security forces launched a rescue operation and evacuated everyone safely by late evening.
The sudden surge in river currents also disrupted movement in other parts of the hill state. Officials said no casualties have been reported.
Kedarnath Yatra temporarily halted
Heavy rainfall and adverse weather conditions forced authorities to suspend the Kedarnath Yatra as a precaution. Pilgrims already en route were advised to wait at designated stops until weather clears. The decision came after several stretches of the trek received intense downpour, increasing the risk of landslides.
UP, Bengal see relief rain
Several Uttar Pradesh districts received moderate showers. Ballia recorded 34.4 mm of rain, Moradabad 21.8 mm, and Lucknow 2.4 mm. The state capital’s maximum temperature dropped to 36.3°C – nearly 4°C below normal – while the minimum settled at 24.7°C.
In Kolkata, afternoon rain accompanied by strong winds flipped umbrellas and caused waterlogging in low-lying areas. Pushkar in Rajasthan also saw heavy rain, leading to similar water accumulation on streets.
Monsoon to reach Kerala by June 4, says IMD
The IMD has forecast that the southwest monsoon is likely to arrive in Kerala around June 4. However, the agency warned that the upcoming rainy season may be weaker than normal. India could receive about 78 cm of rainfall between June and September, below the long-term average of 87 cm.
While Bihar and Uttar Pradesh may get near-normal rain, several other regions – particularly rain-fed agricultural zones – face the risk of below-normal precipitation.
What to expect on June 2 and 3
Strong winds of 40–50 kmph are likely on June 2 over Punjab, Haryana, Delhi, Uttar Pradesh, Jharkhand and Rajasthan. Maharashtra and Karnataka may witness thunderstorms with lightning and gusty winds. Widespread rainfall is expected across Tamil Nadu, Kerala and Karnataka.
On June 3, thunderstorms with similar wind speeds are forecast for Punjab, Haryana, Delhi, Chandigarh, UP and Rajasthan, with dust storms likely in some areas. Madhya Pradesh, Chhattisgarh, Jharkhand and Bihar could see rain and lightning. The northeast will continue to receive heavy to very heavy showers.
